questions.q: Explain the concept of alternative centers of power in the Indian political system. What are the different types of these centers of power and how do they function? (5 marks)
questions.answer: Alternative centers of power refer to the institutions and mechanisms that exercise power and influence in the Indian political system, independent of the formal institutions of government. The different types of alternative centers of power include interest groups, pressure groups, mass media, and civil society organizations. These centers of power function by influencing public opinion, shaping policy decisions, and holding the government accountable for its actions.
marking_scheme: Explain the concept of alternative centers of power (1 mark)
Describe the different types of alternative centers of power (2 marks)

questions.q: Distinguish between interest groups and pressure groups. How do they influence the policy-making process in India? (5 marks)
questions.answer: Interest groups are organizations that represent the interests of a specific group or community, while pressure groups are organizations that seek to influence public policy on a particular issue. Both interest groups and pressure groups influence the policy-making process in India by lobbying with government officials, mobilizing public opinion, and participating in elections.
marking_scheme: Distinguish between interest groups and pressure groups (2 marks)
Explain how interest groups influence the policy-making process (2 marks)
Explain how pressure groups influence the policy-making process (1 mark)

questions.q: A civil society organization in India has a membership of 10,000 people and is active in 20 districts. If 70% of the members are women, calculate the number of women members and the number of districts where women members are more than men. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Calculation: Number of women members = (70/100) x 10,000 = 7,000. Number of districts where women members are more than men = 20 - 10 = 10 (assuming equal number of men and women members in remaining 10 districts).
marking_scheme: Calculate the number of women members (2 marks)
Calculate the number of districts where women members are more than men (3 marks)

questions.q: A mass media organization in India has a readership of 1 million people. If 30% of the readers are from urban areas and 25% are from rural areas, calculate the number of readers from urban and rural areas. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Calculation: Number of readers from urban areas = (30/100) x 1,000,000 = 300,000. Number of readers from rural areas = (25/100) x 1,000,000 = 250,000.
marking_scheme: Calculate the number of readers from urban areas (2 marks)
Calculate the number of readers from rural areas (3 marks)

questions.q: A pressure group in India is planning to organize a rally in the capital city. If the group expects 50% of its members to participate and the total number of members is 50,000, calculate the number of members expected to participate in the rally. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Calculation: Number of members expected to participate = (50/100) x 50,000 = 25,000.
marking_scheme: Calculate the number of members expected to participate (5 marks)

questions.q: Explain the role of civil society organizations in promoting social justice in India. How do they contribute to the empowerment of marginalized communities? (5 marks)
questions.answer: Civil society organizations play a crucial role in promoting social justice in India by advocating for the rights of marginalized communities, providing them with legal and financial support, and empowering them to take control of their lives. They contribute to the empowerment of marginalized communities by providing them with education, healthcare, and economic opportunities.
marking_scheme: Explain the role of civil society organizations in promoting social justice (2 marks)
Describe how they contribute to the empowerment of marginalized communities (3 marks)
